results.title: Histamine elevation in familial Mediterranean fever: A study from the Juvenile Inflammatory Rheumatism cohort.
results.abstract: Familial Mediterranean Fever (FMF) is the most frequent monogenic autoinflammatory disease (AID). Some patients have persistent symptoms despite colchicine intake. Mast cells (MC) are innate immune cells involved in inflammatory conditions including AID. Their activation is responsible for various symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating and pruritus.
